Title: Product Manager
Description: Our Enterprise Technology Division is looking for a Product Manager. The ideal candidate is interested in pursuing a career in Product Development or Enterprise Applications. You are driven, ambitious, and possess excellent problem-solving skills coupled with the ability to work well within a team to deliver smart solutions. Each member of Enterprise Technology Division is a critical contributor to the success of our products. Your valuable contributions will be used daily by our passionate Equinox members and staff. This position is a full-time position located at our NYC Corporate Office. The Product Manager will work under the guidance of a Director and will be tasked with the following: Responsibilities: * Establish a long-term vision, set goals, and build a roadmap that positions the team for success * Create user stories, establish acceptance criteria, manage tickets and help troubleshoot complex issues * Liaise with teams across the organization and manage relationships with business partners and vendors * Collaborate with Engineering, Design, Analytics and QA to deliver solutions that meet customer needs and priorities. Qualifications: 5+ years experience with Salesforce focus, entrepreneurial attitude, data-driven decision making, communication skills, passion for health/fitness. Preferred: Project management/Scrum, product launch track record, enterprise apps (SAP, APIs, SQL), payment processing. Pay: $110K-$120K. Hybrid role (4 days on-site).

Title: Product Manager, Commerce Financials, Apple Services Engineering
Location: New York City, New York, United States
Description: The Apple Services Engineering commerce group is looking for an extraordinary, hands on Product Manager to join our financials product team to enhance our commerce platform. This role will work cross functionally with teams including Commerce Product, Global Finance stakeholders, Technical Accounting, and our Commerce Engineering teams to understand our financial obligations, needs, and data flows to develop an end to end solution for the platform. Responsibilities: Job responsibilities will include cultivation of ideas, authoring requirements, prioritization, and roadmap planning of financial capabilities in support of the platform. In addition, the product management role covers technical analyses of existing service implementation, end-user usability and experience, market analysis, and competitive analysis. Minimum Qualifications: 5+ years of experience working in the commerce space, including technical and business expertise. 2+ years product management experience launching new features working closely with designers and engineers. Expertise in driving customer experience, growth and engagement for digital services. Understanding of financial impact and revenue management. Managing multiple performance obligations would be a plus. Experience in driving system architectural discussions and driving a scalable platform development. BA/BS degree required. Business Information Technology or Finance/Accounting specialization is a plus. Preferred Qualifications: Focus on customers as well as internal partners. Proven ability to meet deadlines and drive results while juggling many priorities and under tight timeframes. Comfortable with deadline driven work environment. Strong prioritization skills and ability to manage multiple programs simultaneously. Expertise in enterprise application like SAP is a plus. Pay & Benefits: At Apple, base pay is one part of our total compensation package and is determined within a range. This provides the opportunity to progress as you grow and develop within a role. The base pay range for this role is between $141,800 and $258,600, and your base pay will depend on your skills, qualifications, experience, and location. Apple employees also have the opportunity to become an Apple shareholder through participation in Apple's discretionary employee stock programs. Apple employees are eligible for discretionary restricted stock unit awards, and can purchase Apple stock at a discount if voluntarily participating in Apple's Employee Stock Purchase Plan. You'll also receive benefits including: Comprehensive medical and dental coverage, retirement benefits, a range of discounted products and free services, and for formal education related to advancing your career at Apple, reimbursement for certain educational expenses — including tuition. Additionally, this role might be eligible for discretionary bonuses or commission payments as well as relocation.
